environment.host: OC2A (Mac Mini M4 Pro 48GB unified RAM)

summary.verdict_explanation: gemma4:31b pulls and runs on OC2A, but two material issues: (1) severe memory pressure pushing system to 0.1% free during long contexts with 111k swapouts observed; (2) every generate response in this test was empty — the model produces 80-200 tokens that decode to empty string, a known symptom of Q4_K_M quant on gemma4 generating control tokens. Throughput is also ~5x slower than 26B (11.5 t/s vs 57.4 t/s). For local Verify/governance use, 26B is materially better on every dimension except raw parameter count.
key_findings: Model on disk: 19 GB (vs 17 GB for 26B; +12%)
Pull time: 833s = 13.9 min, effective bandwidth ~22.8 MB/s
Resident size in VRAM/RAM: 22 GB (vs ~12 GB for 26B)
Cold load: 16.1s (warm load: ~270ms after first call)
Short prompt (100 token in / 80 token out): 11.5 t/s (vs 57.4 t/s on 26B; 5.0x slower)
Medium prompt (1100 token in / 200 token out): 10.75 t/s (vs 56.4 t/s on 26B; 5.2x slower)
Long context (5,957 token in / 100 token out): 9.95 t/s (vs 51.8 t/s on 26B; 5.2x slower)
Concurrent 2-request: both 200 OK, per-request ~11.5 t/s (serialized, ~17s total)
System memory pressure during long context: Pages free = 3,944 / 3,145,728 = 0.1% (CRITICAL)
Swapouts observed during test: 111,016 swapouts and 969 swapins (system was paging)
Unload reclaim: clean, system returns to 87% free (vs 26B ~25.9 GB reclaim)
Empty response bug: ALL generate calls returned empty string despite eval_count tokens being produced; this is a critical functional defect for production use

warmup.observation: Massive memory pressure spike on load: 22GB model + 37GB wired + 2.1GB compressor. System entered active swap (111k swapouts).

benchmark_1_short_prompt.input_prompt: Explain the concept of technical debt in three sentences.
benchmark_1_short_prompt.observation: CRITICAL DEFECT: All three runs returned empty response string despite 80 tokens being generated. eval_count is correct, eval_duration is reasonable, but decoded output is empty. Per-call latency 7.6-8.3s due to model resident and prompt eval overhead. Throughput 5x slower than 26B.

benchmark_2_medium_prompt.observation: Same empty response defect. Prompt eval throughput jumps to 90 t/s on warm (run 2), but generation throughput is still ~10.7 t/s. 5.2x slower than 26B on this workload.

benchmark_3_long_context.description: Long-context memory stress test, ~5,957-token input, num_predict=100

memory_pressure_during.verdict: CRITICAL: 0.1% free pages during eval. 111k swapouts indicate active paging. System functionally responsive but at risk of OOM or stall under additional load.

benchmark_3_long_context.observation: Long context is the most stressful test. System came within 0.1% of OOM during the eval phase. Any additional concurrent service or larger context would risk failure. Same empty response defect. Throughput 9.95 t/s — 5.2x slower than 26B.

concurrent_test.description: Two overlapping short requests, same prompt as Bench 1
concurrent_test.observation: Both requests succeeded (200 OK). Ollama serialized them — R2 took 17s waiting for R1. Per-request throughput held at ~11.5 t/s. No OOM, no failures. Same empty response defect on both.

empty_response_bug.description: CRITICAL functional defect — all test generations returned empty response strings
evidence: Diagnostic 'What is 2+2?': eval_count=20, response='' (even at temperature=0)
Diagnostic with logprobs=true: same — empty response
empty_response_bug.diagnosis: The Q4_K_M quantization of gemma4:31b on Ollama 0.32.1 produces tokens that decode to empty/control characters. This is consistent with a known class of issues in llama.cpp/ollama where the logits collapse to control tokens (low-entropy sampling, sampling parameter issue, or model file corruption). The 26B does NOT have this problem per TKT-1014.
root_cause_hypotheses: 1. Model file corruption on disk (untested — could be re-verified with `ollama show --modelfile` or model SHA check)
2. Quantization-specific decoder issue in Ollama 0.32.1 for 31B (gemma4 family may have wider vocabulary requiring different Q4_K_M parameters)
3. Tokenizer/special-token mismatch between Ollama 0.32.1 and gemma4:31b Q4_K_M weights
4. Sampling default producing EOS-like tokens immediately at every step (though done_reason is 'length' not 'stop')
empty_response_bug.production_impact: FATAL for any production use. The model generates tokens (incurring compute, latency, memory) but produces no usable text. Cannot be used for Verify, governance, or any output-requiring task until resolved.
remediation_options: Re-pull the model: `ollama rm gemma4:31b && ollama pull gemma4:31b` to rule out file corruption
Try alternative quantization: gemma4:31b-q8_0 or gemma4:31b-instruct-q4_K_S if available
Try gemma3:31b (previous gen) to confirm whether bug is in 31B family or specific to v4
Update Ollama to latest: `brew upgrade ollama` and re-test
Set explicit repeat_penalty, top_k, top_p to break sampling degeneracy
Test with different num_ctx (e.g., 8192) to rule out context-length effect

unload_and_recovery.description: Natural unload after 5-minute idle (Ollama default keep_alive)
unload_and_recovery.observation: Clean unload. Reclaim was complete — system returned to 87% free, well above the 26B baseline. This is positive: 31B doesn't permanently fragment memory, it just peaks high during use.

rows.metric: System memory free during long context (%)

rows.31b: GO-WITH-CAVEATS (effectively NO-GO due to empty response bug)

system_capacity_assessment.headline: OC2A 48GB unified RAM is INSUFFICIENT for gemma4:31b in production
evidence: 31B model + OS + Ollama runtime requires ~38GB at peak (22GB resident + 16GB OS/services)
48GB total leaves only ~10GB headroom, which is consumed by KV cache during long context
During Bench 3 (5.9K tokens), system dropped to 0.1% free pages and triggered 111,016 swapouts
Any concurrent production workload (notebook, browser, IDE) would risk OOM kill
boundary_conditions: Short context (≤2K tokens): usable, 17% free, but tight
Medium context (2K-5K tokens): borderline, 0-5% free, swap I/O active
Long context (5K+ tokens): NOT SAFE for production, system at OOM risk
system_capacity_assessment.production_impact: Cannot be used as default local governance/Verify model. 26B is the practical ceiling on 48GB.

for_local_production_use.rationale: 26B outperforms 31B on every measurable dimension: 5x faster throughput, 2x faster cold load, 0.7x resident memory, 0 swapouts, no empty-response bug. The 31B's only advantage (slightly larger parameter count) is moot because the model produces no usable output and the system cannot safely run it under realistic context loads.

recommendation.for_31b_specifically: NO-GO. The empty-response bug is fatal for any production use. Even after a fix, the memory pressure on 48GB makes it unsafe for anything beyond toy workloads.
next_steps: 1. Do NOT route any production traffic to gemma4:31b until the empty-response bug is root-caused and fixed.
2. File a follow-up: re-test after re-pulling the model and/or upgrading Ollama.
3. Keep gemma4:26b as the primary local governance/Verify model (TKT-1014 verdict: GO).
4. If 31B is required for a specific use case, consider: (a) upgrading OC2A to 64GB+ RAM, or (b) running 31B on a dedicated server (e.g., AWS g5.12xlarge with 192GB RAM and dedicated A10G GPU).
5. Consider testing gemma3:31b-instruct-q4_K_M as a workaround — gemma3 family is well-tested and unlikely to have the decoder bug.
6. The 31B model files (19GB) can remain on disk — they do not impact the 26B workload and represent sunk test cost.
